heading: Compensation for work injuries; generally
label: 8171 Compensation for work injuries; generally
notes: Historical and Revision NotesDerivationU.S. CodeRevised Statutes andStatutes at Large 5 U.S.C. 150k–1(a)., .June 19, 1952, ch. 444, § 266 Stat. 139, “Sec. 2(a)”, .July 18, 1958Pub. L. 85–538, § 172 Stat. 397In subsection (a), the word “civilian” is omitted as unnecessary as the definition of “employee” in section 2105 includes only civilians.In subsection (d), the reference to “the United States District Court for the District of Columbia” is omitted as included in the words “district court”.Standard changes are made to conform with the definitions applicable and the style of this title as outlined in the preface to the report.

(a), is , , which is classified generally to chapter 18 (§ 901 et seq.) of Title 33, Navigation and Navigable Waters. children.text: a United States citizen or a permanent resident of the United States or a territory or possession of the United States employed outside the continental United States; or
employed inside the continental United States.

children.label: b
children.text: For the purpose of this subchapter, the term “employer” in section 2(4) of the Longshore and Harbor Workers’ Compensation Act () includes the nonappropriated fund instrumentalities described by .33 U.S.C. 902(4)section 2105(c) of this title

extend compensation districts established under section 39(b) of the Longshore and Harbor Workers’ Compensation Act (), or establish new districts to include the areas outside the continental United States; and33 U.S.C. 939(b)
assign to each district one or more deputy commissioners as the Secretary considers advisable.

children.label: d
children.text: Judicial proceedings under sections 18 and 21 of the Longshore and Harbor Workers’ Compensation Act ( and 921) with respect to an injury or death occurring outside the continental United States shall be instituted in the district court within the territorial jurisdiction of which is located the office of the deputy commissioner having jurisdiction with respect to the injury or death.33 U.S.C. 918
